notes: SYS.* are publish-EDGE runtime/integrity codes emitted by engine/orchestrator/publish-executor.js (and the readback verifier) onto the queue entry / event ledger — NOT content-gate detections over a draft/package, so the gate-regression runner does not replay them through a content gate (executed_in_ci:false). Each case is a queue-entry-shaped fixture (schema-valid against queue-entry.schema.json) capturing the post-trigger state, plus the executor condition that emits the code. The live crash-safety scenarios are exercised by the executor coverage suite (plan P4-COV-ORCH); here they pin the SYS.* contract and give registry-integrity coverage of every SYS code. All SYS.* are HARD/block, route publisher-liaison.

cases.name: test-publish-blocked
cases.trigger: A content item whose id marks it a test fixture (content_id begins 'test-') reaches the publish edge.
cases.notes: Fail-closed test guard: the executor refuses to publish a test-id item.

cases.name: retry-exhausted
cases.trigger: The hard-fail retry bound (3, DD-13) is exhausted; attempt_count reached 3.
cases.notes: Item dead-letters; an 'unfilled slot' notice is emitted. Attempt counter durably incremented before each retry's spend.

cases.name: handoff-failed
cases.trigger: The publisher adapter handoff errored (external publish call failed); within the retry bound.
cases.notes: Idempotent retry within the bound applies; on exhaustion becomes SYS.RETRY_EXHAUSTED.

cases.name: interrupted-mid-publish
cases.trigger: The executor was interrupted mid-publish (crash/timeout) with a write-ahead intent persisted.
cases.notes: Entry enters interrupted_hold for crash-safe replay rather than risking a double-post.

cases.name: readback-fail
cases.trigger: The live approval card read back does not match the card the engine built (foreign edit / render corruption).
cases.notes: Integrity check fails closed — the item does not advance.
